item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What makes you think he was talking to them? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then what did it mean when the monster said that it thought that the two who came into the dream loved it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just having a good time? That shouldn't have come across at all, hmm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, as there's this great charm-in-human-faults thing going on and, as others have said, the setup is a good way to make a dream-based SCP work.

I admit to being utterly stumped by the monster and it's seemingly, "Oh shit, I thought we were just having a good time!" behavior (as hilarious as the mental image of that thing doing that is). Same with the clearing sky. Maybe I'll just read it again tomorrow and it'll click.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty much just the difference between sleeping the night and waking up in a cold sweat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm usually not a big fan of dream skips, but I rather enjoyed this one. The human story of the two researchers was nice and the mystery of the table's origin/purpose hits that sweet spot of just enough explanation.

+1, and I'd upvote twice if I could simply for not making a "if u die in tha dream u die for real" SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems McCoy is gay or at least asexual. Not in to women at any right. Sawyer was affected by the scip and fell for McCoy, but is married to a woman, so I'd hazard to say she's bisexual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed it but I'm experiencing some degree of confusion.

Is the chase entirely superfluous? Is there any difference in outcome between the two -B's being caught or evading the -A? Or does it literally only mean they wake up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, "it got a lot gayer" is one of the best things I can say about how the site's changed over time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On a somewhat related note: obviously not saying this like it's a bad thing, but I literally cannot recall the last time I saw a romantic pairing on this site between two heterosexual characters, lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bisexuality exists. Dr. Sawyer might be bi or pan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a lesbian and a homosexual get in bed together…

Is that right or is McCoy asexual?

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to daveyoufool and spookman (don't know your wiki account) for feedback.

+ Commentary
- Commentary

This one's been in the works for a while. It is two things.

First, it's a tribute to the music of Zedd, most especially Find You and Spectrum (though I recommend the eurobeat version), with a little Clarity thrown in at the end. I've written fanfiction based on music before and wanted to see if I could apply the same technique to SCPs. Time will tell if it's successful.

Secondly, this is a reaction and do-it-better of one of my biggest pet peeves: dream skips. I absolutely detest dream SCPs because there's no way to independently verify what happens to test subjects in them. So here we have something that plays the dreams for you instead of the sleeper having to remember them.

As for the story behind this, I think I'll keep it close to my chest and let the article speak for itself.

EDIT: Also this is now SCP-2676, because that's apparently a thing you can do and I'm mega-OCD.
